title: Verifying RBAC Permissions with kubectl auth can-i
description:
## Verifying RBAC Permissions with `kubectl auth can-i`

`kubectl auth can-i` lets you check whether the **current user** (or impersonate another user/ServiceAccount) can perform a specific action — without actually performing it. This is essential for auditing RBAC configurations.

Syntax:
```bash
kubectl auth can-i <verb> <resource> [--namespace=<ns>] [--as=<user>]
```

**Your task:**

1. Create a ServiceAccount named `audit-sa` in the namespace `audit-ns`
2. Create a Role named `secret-viewer` in `audit-ns` allowing `get` and `list` on `secrets`
3. Create a RoleBinding named `audit-binding` that binds `secret-viewer` to `audit-sa`
4. Verify the permissions using `kubectl auth can-i` impersonating the ServiceAccount:
   - Confirm `audit-sa` **can** `get secrets` in `audit-ns`
   - Confirm `audit-sa` **cannot** `delete secrets` in `audit-ns`

```bash
# Impersonate the ServiceAccount:
kubectl auth can-i get secrets -n audit-ns --as=system:serviceaccount:audit-ns:audit-sa
kubectl auth can-i delete secrets -n audit-ns --as=system:serviceaccount:audit-ns:audit-sa
```

hints.title: Create the namespace and ServiceAccount
hints.body: Create the namespace first, then the ServiceAccount inside it.
hints.command: kubectl create namespace audit-ns && kubectl create serviceaccount audit-sa -n audit-ns

hints.title: Create Role and RoleBinding
hints.body: Use kubectl create role and rolebinding imperatively.
hints.command: kubectl create role secret-viewer --verb=get,list --resource=secrets -n audit-ns && kubectl create rolebinding audit-binding --role=secret-viewer --serviceaccount=audit-ns:audit-sa -n audit-ns

hints.title: Verify with kubectl auth can-i --as
hints.body: The --as flag impersonates a user or ServiceAccount. ServiceAccount format: system:serviceaccount:<namespace>:<name>
hints.command: kubectl auth can-i get secrets -n audit-ns --as=system:serviceaccount:audit-ns:audit-sa

commands.command: kubectl get serviceaccount audit-sa -n audit-ns -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get role secret-viewer -n audit-ns -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].resources[*]}'

commands.description: audit-sa CAN get secrets in audit-ns
commands.command: kubectl auth can-i get secrets -n audit-ns --as=system:serviceaccount:audit-ns:audit-sa
kubectl get rolebinding audit-binding -n audit-ns -o jsonpath='{.subjects[0].name}'

commands.description: Role 'secret-viewer' does not grant delete verb
commands.command: kubectl get role secret-viewer -n audit-ns -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].verbs}'
